3SB - Turnover & Possession Report

Match-level statistics

Points played

Scoring efficiency - points per possession

Passes per successful possession

Average number of passes in a possession that ended in a score. A successful possession is the run of passes after the last turnover (or the whole point, if there were no turnovers) when we scored.

Break rate

Share of points we did not score.

Clean holds

Share of points where we scored without a single turnover.

Recovery - scoring after a turnover

Of points that included at least one turnover, how often did we still score?

Pass-shape analysis

Passes below are normalized to a single offensive direction (left → right). A huck is a pass whose horizontal span covers at least 50% of the in-field (≥ 336 px). A backward pass moves opposite to the normalized attacking direction.

Score-attempt turnover rate

Any pass whose destination lands inside the attacking endzone is treated as a score attempt. For each match: what share of those attempts ended in a turnover.

Hucks per game and their turnover rate

Turnover rate by pass direction

Turnover breakdown - mechanical, huck, other

A turnover counts as mechanical when its comment contains “mechanic” (mechanický, mechanicky, …) and as huck when its horizontal span is at least 50 % of the in-field. No turnover currently falls into both buckets, so the categories are disjoint.

Turnover heat-maps

All maps below use turnover passes after normalization (offense → right). Darker = more.

Origin - where turnovers are thrown from

Destination - where turnovers end up

The intended (or actual) target location of every turnover pass.

Arrows - every turnover, normalized

Each turnover pass drawn as a red arrow from origin to destination, with the attacking direction unified to the right.

Pass-distance distribution

Distribution of pass horizontal span - completed vs. turnovers. Dashed line marks the huck threshold.
